action.reasoning: Observation: The dilated checkpoint still underperforms badly after fixing predict parity (482 vs best 464), so the issue is not just inference mismatch; meanwhile the non-dilated residual CNN improved consistently with longer training from 465.7 to 464.0. Analysis: The safest path to improve rmse_z500 is to return to the proven architecture and continue optimization rather than spend more time on risky new models. With ~3.45h left, one more longer continuation run on the best non-dilated model is affordable. Decision: restore model defaults to the proven non-dilated setting, rewrite train.py cleanly and fully, add resume support from best.pt, continue training to 32 total epochs with a lower LR, smoke-test predict locally, and submit the updated best checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 32 --batch_size 64 --lr 3e-4 --min_lr 5e-5 --hidden 160 --depth 16 --dropout 0.05 --num_workers 4 --use_coords --dilation_cycle 1 --resume checkpoints/best.pt --save_path checkpoints/best.pt && python predict.py --data_path local_testdata2 --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output local_testdata2/out_resume32.json
